properties.name: BRIC House
properties.description: "A leading arts and media institution anchored in Downtown Brooklyn whose work spans contemporary visual and performing arts, media, and civic action." (bricartsmedia.org)
properties.note: Gallery open 11AM–6PM Wed–Sun.

properties.name: China Institute
properties.description: "China Institute Gallery is a center of Chinese art in New York. Established in 1966, China Institute Gallery was the first non-profit gallery in the United States to showcase Chinese art and culture exclusively and on a regular basis, now located in Lower Manhattan." (Via chinainstitute.org)

properties.name: China Institute
properties.description: "China Institute Gallery is a center of Chinese art in New York. Established in 1966, China Institute Gallery was the first non-profit gallery in the United States to showcase Chinese art and culture exclusively and on a regular basis, now located in Lower Manhattan." (Via chinainstitute.org)

properties.name: Dia Chelsea
properties.description: "Dia Art Foundation is committed to advancing, realizing, and preserving the vision of artists." (Via diaart.org)

properties.name: Dr. Bernard Heller Museum
properties.description: "The Heller Museum, located in New York City’s Greenwich Village at the Hebrew Union College-Jewish Institute of Religion, showcases the creativity of contemporary artists of all faiths who explore and comment on culture, history, and current social issues." (Via huc.edu)
properties.note: Current government issued photo ID required for entry.

properties.name: Drawing Center
properties.description: "The Drawing Center—an exhibition space in downtown Manhattan's SoHo neighborhood—explores the medium of drawing as primary, dynamic, and relevant to contemporary culture, the future of art, and creative thought." (Via drawingcenter.org)

properties.name: Godwin-Ternbach Museum
properties.description: "The Godwin-Ternbach Museum, a part of the Kupferberg Center for the Visual and Performing Arts, located on the Flushing campus of Queens College, City University of New York, organizes cultural exhibitions and programs of contemporary and historical significance for the diverse audiences of Queens and the metropolitan region." (Via gtmuseum.org)

properties.name: Grey Art Museum
properties.description: "The Grey Art Museum, New York University’s fine arts museum, enables and encourages transformative encounters with works of art." (Via greyartmuseum.nyu.edu)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Wednesday, closes 5PM Saturday.

properties.name: Hill Art Foundation
properties.description: "The Hill Art Foundation is a nonprofit organization founded with the goals of amplifying the artist’s voice, deepening the relationship between art and the viewer, and connecting the dots between artistic movements through meaningful juxtapositions." (Via hillartfoundation.org)

properties.name: Lehman College Art Gallery
properties.description: "Lehman College Art Gallery has been serving the interests of our diverse audience from the Bronx and greater New York City since 1984. The gallery specializes in thematic group exhibitions that bring together famous artists with emerging talents." (via lehmangallery.org)

properties.name: Leslie-Lohman Museum of Art
properties.description: "The Leslie-Lohman Museum is a dynamic and safe space where the LGBTQIA+ community and its allies engage in meaningful and authentic art experiences that foster empowerment and community building." (Via lohman.zetcom.net)
properties.note: Closes at 5PM on Wednesday.

properties.name: MoMA PS1
properties.description: "Artist-centered and community-driven, MoMA PS1 embraces boundary-breaking ideas and experimental practices." (Via momaps1.org)

properties.name: Nicholas Roerich Museum
properties.description: "The mission of the Nicholas Roerich Museum is essentially a narrow one: to make available to the public the full range of Roerich’s accomplishments. These, however, are not narrow; they cover the realms of art, science, spirituality, peacemaking, and more. Because Roerich’s activities ranged widely, so do the Museum’s." (Via roerich.org)
properties.note: Open until 5PM on weekends.

properties.name: Salmagundi Club
properties.description: "Salmagundi [pronounced: sal-muh-guhn-dee] is a not-for-profit 501(c)(3) organization created in 1871, by artists and patrons, to support one another and to encourage the advancement of art of all kinds." (Via salmagundi.org)
properties.note: Closes at 5PM on weekends.

properties.name: Sculpture Center
properties.description: "SculptureCenter leads the conversation on contemporary art by supporting artistic innovation and independent thought highlighting sculpture's specific potential to change the way we engage with the world." (Via sculpture-center.org)

properties.name: Sheila C. Johnson Design Center
properties.description: "The mission of the Sheila C. Johnson Design Center (SJDC) is to generate an active dialogue on the role of innovative art and design in responding to our contemporary world." (Via parsons.edu)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Thursday.

properties.name: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
properties.description: "From its opening to the present day, the Guggenheim Museum has been an unparalleled physical and cultural presence in the New York landscape." (Via Guggenheim.org)
properties.note: Advance tickets for Pay-What-You-Wish hours are available online only and released at 10 am the morning of the event.

properties.name: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
properties.description: "From its opening to the present day, the Guggenheim Museum has been an unparalleled physical and cultural presence in the New York landscape." (Via Guggenheim.org)

properties.name: Swiss Institute of Contemporary Art
properties.description: "Swiss Institute is an independent non-profit contemporary art institution dedicated to promoting forward-thinking and experimental art making through innovative exhibitions, education, and programs." (Via swissinstitute.net)
properties.note: Open 12-8PM Saturday and 12-6PM Sunday.

properties.name: The Bronx Museum of the Arts
properties.description: "Since it was founded by community advocates in 1971, The Bronx Museum has been committed to programming that champions contemporary artists whose identities and/or artwork reflect the lived experiences of people residing in the South Bronx." (Via bronxmuseum.org)

properties.name: The Frick Collection
properties.description: "Internationally recognized as a premier museum and research center, the Frick is known for its distinguished Old Master paintings and outstanding examples of European sculpture and decorative arts." (Via Frick.org)
properties.note: Tickets are pay what you wish with a minimum contribution of $1. Advance tickets are required. Also free always for 18 & under.

properties.name: The Frick Collection
properties.description: "Internationally recognized as a premier museum and research center, the Frick is known for its distinguished Old Master paintings and outstanding examples of European sculpture and decorative arts." (Via Frick.org)

properties.name: The Jewish Museum
properties.description: "The Jewish Museum is an art museum committed to illuminating the complexity and vibrancy of Jewish culture for a global audience. Through distinctive exhibitions and programs that present the work of diverse artists and thinkers, we share ideas, provoke dialogue, and promote understanding." (Via thejewishmuseum.org)
properties.note: Advance timed tickets are available online as well as onsite in the Museum’s lobby.

properties.name: The Jewish Museum
properties.description: "The Jewish Museum is an art museum committed to illuminating the complexity and vibrancy of Jewish culture for a global audience. Through distinctive exhibitions and programs that present the work of diverse artists and thinkers, we share ideas, provoke dialogue, and promote understanding." (Via thejewishmuseum.org)
properties.note: Advance timed tickets are available online as well as onsite in the Museum’s lobby. Closing hours vary, check website.

properties.name: The Whitney
properties.description: "The Whitney is the only museum dedicated to American art and artists. Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney founded the Museum in 1930, taking a bold stand for American artists who were often overlooked." (Via whitney.org)
properties.note: Free every Friday and the Second Sunday of every month. Though admission is free, tickets are required and capacity is limited. Advance tickets are recommended. Visitors 25 and under are always free, every day.

properties.name: The Whitney
properties.description: "The Whitney is the only museum dedicated to American art and artists. Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney founded the Museum in 1930, taking a bold stand for American artists who were often overlooked." (Via whitney.org)

properties.name: Wallach Art Gallery
properties.description: "Established in 1986, the Wallach Art Gallery is the University's premier public visual arts space. We are a platform for critically acclaimed exhibitions, a dynamic range of programming, and publications that contribute to scholarship." (Via wallach.columbia.edu)

properties.name: Center for Architecture
properties.description: "The Center for Architecture engages local and international audiences with the value, impact, and wonder of architecture." (via centerforarchitecture.org)
properties.note: Open 11AM-5PM Saturday.

properties.name: Cooper Hewitt
properties.description: "Founded in 1897 by Sarah and Eleanor Hewitt, the granddaughters of industrialist Peter Cooper, Cooper Hewitt, Smithsonian Design Museum advances the public understanding of design through dynamic, interactive exhibitions, stimulating programming, and a broad array of online learning resources." (Via Cooperhewitt.org)
properties.note: Also free all day for 18 & under.

properties.name: Cooper Hewitt
properties.description: "Founded in 1897 by Sarah and Eleanor Hewitt, the granddaughters of industrialist Peter Cooper, Cooper Hewitt, Smithsonian Design Museum advances the public understanding of design through dynamic, interactive exhibitions, stimulating programming, and a broad array of online learning resources." (Via Cooperhewitt.org)

properties.name: Grolier Club
properties.description: "Founded in 1884, the Grolier Club is America’s oldest and largest society for bibliophiles and enthusiasts in the graphic arts." (Via grolierclub.org)
properties.note: Gallery open to public, library hours by appointment only.

properties.name: Museum at the Fashion Institute of Technology
properties.description: "The Museum at FIT, accredited by the American Alliance of Museums, is one of a select group of specialized fashion museums, including the Musée de la Mode, the Mode Museum, and the Museo de la Moda." (Via fitnyc.edu)
properties.note: Open 10AM-5PM weekends.

properties.name: Museum of the Moving Image
properties.description: "Museum of the Moving Image is the only museum in the U.S. that explores the central technology of the present moment. The moving image—encompassing film, television, video games, and other forms of digital media—shapes how we see and feel the world." (Via movingimage.org)
properties.note: No reservation required.

properties.name: Museum of the Moving Image
properties.description: "Museum of the Moving Image is the only museum in the U.S. that explores the central technology of the present moment. The moving image—encompassing film, television, video games, and other forms of digital media—shapes how we see and feel the world." (Via movingimage.org)
properties.note: Hours vary, check website.

properties.name: New York Public Library for the Performing Arts
properties.description: "The New York Public Library for the Performing Arts, one of NYPL’s renowned research libraries, houses one of the world's most extensive research collections in the fields of theater, film, dance, music, and recorded sound, as well as a wide array of circulating and reference materials." (Via nypl.org)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Thursday.

properties.name: Poster House
properties.description: "Poster House is dedicated to presenting the impact, culture, and design of posters, both as historical documents and methods of contemporary visual communication." (Via posterhouse.org)
properties.note: Free entry every friday and on the third Sunday of every month. Closes 6PM Sunday.

properties.name: Poster House
properties.description: "Poster House is dedicated to presenting the impact, culture, and design of posters, both as historical documents and methods of contemporary visual communication." (Via posterhouse.org)
properties.note: Open until 9PM Fridays.

properties.name: American Folk Art Museum
properties.description: "Candid, genuine, and unexpected, we are the nation’s museum of folk and self-taught artists. Since 1961, the Museum has celebrated the creativity of individuals whose singular talents have been refined largely through personal experience rather than formal artistic training." (via folkartmuseum.org)

properties.name: Print Center New York
properties.description: "A nonprofit organization, Print Center New York champions printmaking as an art form that drives invention, collaboration, and access, and plays a vital role in society." (via printcenternewyork.org)

properties.name: Brooklyn Museum
properties.description: "For 200 years, the Brooklyn Museum has been recognized as a trailblazer. Through a vast array of exhibitions, public programs, and community-centered initiatives, it continues to broaden the narratives of art, uplift a multitude of voices, and center creative expression within important dialogues of the day." (via brooklynmuseum.org).
properties.note: Fully free the first Saturday of every month. Advance resgistration required.

properties.name: Brooklyn Museum
properties.description: "For 200 years, the Brooklyn Museum has been recognized as a trailblazer. Through a vast array of exhibitions, public programs, and community-centered initiatives, it continues to broaden the narratives of art, uplift a multitude of voices, and center creative expression within important dialogues of the day." (via brooklynmuseum.org).

properties.name: Neue Galerie
properties.description: "Neue Galerie New York is a museum devoted to early twentieth-century German and Austrian art and design." (via neuegalerie.org)
properties.note: Free the first Friday of every month. Tickets are first-come, first-served.

properties.name: Noguchi Museum
properties.description: "Founded in 1985 by category-defying artist Isamu Noguchi, The Noguchi Museum, was the first museum in the United States to be established, designed, and installed by a living artist to show their own work." (via noguchi.org).
properties.note: Free the first Friday of every month. Advance reservations not required.

properties.name: Noguchi Museum
properties.description: "Founded in 1985 by category-defying artist Isamu Noguchi, The Noguchi Museum, was the first museum in the United States to be established, designed, and installed by a living artist to show their own work." (via noguchi.org).

properties.name: Museum of Chinese in America
properties.description: "The Museum of Chinese in America is dedicated to preserving, illuminating, and celebrating the rich history and contributions of Chinese in America. By fostering understanding and greater recognition of our experiences, we endeavor to inspire and connect all Americans." (Via mocanyc.org)
properties.note: Closes 4PM Sunday. Admission is Pay-What-You-Wish.

properties.name: Museum of Chinese in America
properties.description: "The Museum of Chinese in America is dedicated to preserving, illuminating, and celebrating the rich history and contributions of Chinese in America. By fostering understanding and greater recognition of our experiences, we endeavor to inspire and connect all Americans." (Via mocanyc.org)
properties.note: Closes 4PM Sunday.

properties.name: National Museum of the American Indian
properties.description: "A diverse and multifaceted cultural and educational enterprise, the National Museum of the American Indian is an active and visible component of the Smithsonian Institution, the world's largest museum complex." (Via americanindian.si.edu)

properties.name: Stonewall National Monument Visitor Center
properties.description: "The mission of the Stonewall National Monument Visitor Center is to memorialize the legacy of the Stonewall Rebellion while serving as a beacon for generations to come and visit the very site where history was made and the fight for full LGBTQ+ equality began." (Via stonewallvisitorcenter.org)

properties.name: Asia Society
properties.description: "Asia Society fosters insight, encourages engagement, elevates regional voices, informs and educates new audiences, addresses complex – and where necessary – contentious challenges, engages in creative problem-solving, and helps deliver real solutions for the benefit of all." (Via asiasociety.org)

properties.name: Asia Society
properties.description: "Asia Society fosters insight, encourages engagement, elevates regional voices, informs and educates new audiences, addresses complex – and where necessary – contentious challenges, engages in creative problem-solving, and helps deliver real solutions for the benefit of all." (Via asiasociety.org)

properties.name: Austrian Cultural Forum
properties.description: "Located in a critically acclaimed landmark building in the heart of Manhattan, the Austrian Cultural Forum New York is Austria’s leading cultural representation in the United States. The ACFNY presents highlights of Austrian culture in the US by organizing exhibitions and events in the fields of visual art, music, film, theater and literature. All of these are open and free to the public." (Via acfny.org)

properties.name: Bernard Museum of Judaica
properties.description: "The exquisite artifacts of community and ceremonial life that make up the Bernard Museum’s permanent collection offer a vivid perspective on the diversity of Jewish culture through the ages." (Via emanuelnyc.org)

properties.name: Czech Center
properties.description: "The mission of the Czech Center New York (CCNY) is to actively promote the Czech Republic by highlighting its rich culture, innovation, and creativity in the United States." (Via new-york.czechcentres.cz)

properties.name: Kehila Kedosha Janina Synagogue and Museum
properties.description: "[The Synagogue] houses a museum about Greek Jewry that is open to the public every Sunday, as well as by appointment. The museum serves as a repository for Romaniote and Sephardic Greek Jewish history, both in Greece and on the Lower East Side." (Via kkjsm.org)

properties.name: Museum at Eldridge Street Synagogue
properties.description: "The Museum at Eldridge Street is housed in the historic Eldridge Street Synagogue. Built in 1887, it is an architectural marvel, and a symbol of immigrant aspirations realized." (Via Eldridgestreet.org)
properties.note: Tickets are pay what you wish Mondays and Fridays. Advance registration is strongly encouraged.

properties.name: Museum at Eldridge Street Synagogue
properties.description: "The Museum at Eldridge Street is housed in the historic Eldridge Street Synagogue. Built in 1887, it is an architectural marvel, and a symbol of immigrant aspirations realized." (Via Eldridgestreet.org)

properties.name: Museum of Reclaimed Urban Space
properties.description: "The Museum of Reclaimed Urban Space (MoRUS) was founded in 2012 by members of Times Up! environmental organization and based in C-Squat. As a living history of urban activism, MoRUS chronicles the LES community’s history of grassroots action." (Via morusnyc.org)

properties.name: New York Public Library Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture
properties.description: "The Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture in Harlem is one of The New York Public Library’s renowned research libraries. A world-leading cultural institution devoted to the research, preservation, and exhibition of materials focused on African American, African Diasporic, and African experiences, the Schomburg stewards a collection of over 11 million items." (Via nypl.org)

properties.name: The Africa Cenrer
properties.description: "The Africa Center is transforming the world’s understanding of Africa, its Diaspora, and the role of people of African descent in the world. Serving as the hub for the exchange of ideas related to the continent, and in the spirit of collaboration with individuals and institutions who share our values, The Africa Center inspires enthusiasm, advances thought, and empowers action around Africa’s global impact on our collective futures." (Via theafricacenter.org)

properties.name: Hispanic Society of America
properties.description: "The Hispanic Society of America was founded in 1904 by Archer Milton Huntington (1870-1955) with the object of establishing a free, public museum and reference library for the study of the art and culture of the Spain, Portugal, Latin America, and the Philippines." (Via hispanicsociety.org)
properties.note: Reading room hours differ from general hours, check website.

properties.name: National Jazz Museum in Harlem
properties.description: "The National Jazz Museum in Harlem (NJMH) is on a mission to preserve, promote and present jazz by inspiring knowledge, appreciation and the celebration of jazz locally, nationally and internationally." (Via jmih.org)

properties.name: El Museo del Barrio
properties.description: "The mission of El Museo del Barrio is to present and preserve the art and culture of Puerto Ricans and all Latin Americans in the United States." (via elmuseo.org)
properties.note: Admission is Pay-What-You-Can.

properties.name: Museum of the City of New York
properties.description: "The Museum of the City of New York fosters understanding of the distinctive nature of urban life in the world’s most influential metropolis." (via www.mcny.org)
properties.note: Open until 6PM on weekends. Free Wednesday tickets only available at ticket counters. Also always free for 18 & under.

properties.name: Museum of the City of New York
properties.description: "The Museum of the City of New York fosters understanding of the distinctive nature of urban life in the world’s most influential metropolis." (via www.mcny.org)
properties.note: Open until 6PM on weekends.

properties.name: Queens Museum
properties.description: "The Queens Museum is dedicated to presenting high quality arts and educational programming for the people of New York, and particularly the residents of Queens, a uniquely diverse ethnic, cultural, and international community." (via queensmuseum.org)
properties.note: Opens 11AM on weekends. Reserve a timed entry ticket prior to visiting.

properties.name: Japan Society
properties.description: "Japan Society is the premier organization connecting Japanese arts, culture, business, and society with audiences in New York and around the world." (via japansociety.org)
properties.note: Free the first Friday of every month.

properties.name: Japan Society
properties.description: "Japan Society is the premier organization connecting Japanese arts, culture, business, and society with audiences in New York and around the world." (via japansociety.org)
properties.note: Open 12-6PM weekends.

properties.name: High Line
properties.description: "The High Line is both a nonprofit organization and a public park on the West Side of Manhattan. Through our work with communities on and off the High Line, we’re devoted to reimagining the role public spaces have in creating connected, healthy neighborhoods and cities." (Via thehighline.org)
properties.note: The High Line’s hours vary by season: December 1 – March 31: 7AM to 8PM, April 1 – November 30: 7AM to 10PM

properties.name: Little Island
properties.description: "Founded in 2021, Little Island is an award-winning public park, designed as an entirely new type of public space for New York, one that would create an immersive experience with nature and art." (Via littleisland.org)
properties.note: Closing hours vary seasonally, check website.

properties.name: New York Botanical Garden
properties.description: "The New York Botanical Garden is a museum of plants, a center for learning, and a plant science leader in the Bronx." (Via nybg.org)
properties.note: Free entry does not include entry to the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory, special exhibitions, or Tram Tour.

properties.name: New York Botanical Garden
properties.description: "The New York Botanical Garden is a museum of plants, a center for learning, and a plant science leader in the Bronx." (Via nybg.org)
properties.note: Free entry does not include entry to the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory, special exhibitions, or Tram Tour.

properties.name: New York Botanical Garden
properties.description: "The New York Botanical Garden is a museum of plants, a center for learning, and a plant science leader in the Bronx." (Via nybg.org)

properties.name: Queens County Farm Museum
properties.description: "Queens Farm’s 47-acre tract of farmland exemplifies the 300-year history of agriculture and farming as a way of life in New York City." (Via queensfarm.org)
properties.note: Hours for the Farmstand, Farm Store and grounds may differ on ticketed event days and holidays.

properties.name: Snug Harbor Cultural Center & Botanical Garden
properties.description: "Snug Harbor Cultural Center & Botanical Garden is a place where history, architecture, gardens, agriculture, visual and performing arts, and education come together to provide a dynamic experience for all ages." (Via snug-harbor.org)
properties.note: Open from dawn to dusk. Main grounds are free, but Newhouse Center for Contemporary Art and the Chinese Scholar’s Garden require admission fees and have different hours of operation.

properties.name: Socrates Sculpture Park
properties.description: "Socrates Sculpture Park is a community engaged New York City waterfront park dedicated to supporting artists in the production and presentation of public art." (Via socratessculpturepark.org)

properties.name: Voelker Orth Museum
properties.description: "Through the experience of an  immigrant family’s 1890s home, the Voelker Orth Museum, Bird Sanctuary and Victorian Garden preserves and interprets the cultural and horticultural heritage of Flushing, Queens and adjacent communities to engage their ever-changing populations." (Via vomuseum.org)
properties.note: Opens 12PM Sunday.

properties.name: Wave Hill
properties.description: "An urban oasis, Wave Hill’s 28 acres sit high above the Hudson River with sweeping views and lots of open sky--an enchanting surprise for all the senses. Here nature is the springboard for workshops and walks, talks, contemporary art, live performances and more--there is always something to make, to learn or simply experience, each moment circling back to Wave Hill’s serene setting, inviting us to connect to nature in myriad ways." (Via wavehill.org)

properties.name: Wave Hill
properties.description: "An urban oasis, Wave Hill’s 28 acres sit high above the Hudson River with sweeping views and lots of open sky--an enchanting surprise for all the senses. Here nature is the springboard for workshops and walks, talks, contemporary art, live performances and more--there is always something to make, to learn or simply experience, each moment circling back to Wave Hill’s serene setting, inviting us to connect to nature in myriad ways." (Via wavehill.org)

properties.name: 9/11 Museum
properties.description: "Located at the World Trade Center in New York City, the 9/11 Memorial Museum tells the story of 9/11 through media, narratives, and a collection of monumental and authentic artifacts, presenting visitors with personal stories of loss, recovery, and hope." (Via 911memorial.org)
properties.note: Free Admission Monday tickets will be available on the website each Monday beginning at 7AM and are first come, first serve.

properties.name: 9/11 Museum
properties.description: "Located at the World Trade Center in New York City, the 9/11 Memorial Museum tells the story of 9/11 through media, narratives, and a collection of monumental and authentic artifacts, presenting visitors with personal stories of loss, recovery, and hope." (Via 911memorial.org)
properties.note: Free from 4-7PM the first Sunday of every month for New York City residents.

properties.name: 9/11 Museum
properties.description: "Located at the World Trade Center in New York City, the 9/11 Memorial Museum tells the story of 9/11 through media, narratives, and a collection of monumental and authentic artifacts, presenting visitors with personal stories of loss, recovery, and hope." (Via 911memorial.org)

properties.name: African Burial Ground Memorial Site
properties.description: "African Burial Ground is the oldest and largest known excavated burial ground in North America for both free and enslaved Africans. It protects the historic role slavery played in building New York." (Via nps.gov)

properties.name: Brooklyn Navy Yard BLDG 92
properties.description: "Brooklyn Navy Yard: Past, Present, and Future is a permanent exhibition at Building 92 that tells the story of the Brooklyn Navy Yard from 1801, when it was founded as one of the nation’s first federal shipyards, through to the site’s use today as an active industrial and innovation hub that is home to hundreds of businesses." (Via brooklynnavyyard.org)

properties.name: Castle Clinton National Monument
properties.description: "Located at the southern tip of Manhattan Island, Castle Clinton stands where New York City began, and represents not only the city’s growth, but the growth of a nation. Initially intended to prevent a British invasion in 1812, the fortification has transformed over the years to welcome theatergoers, immigrants, sightseers, and now, millions of visitors to New York Harbor." (Via nps.gov)

properties.name: Center for Brooklyn History
properties.description: "Formerly known as the Brooklyn Historical Society, CBH became part of Brooklyn Public Library in 2020 and is now free and accessible to all for research, education, culture and more. Our freshly renovated landmark building—home to the Othmer Library's magnificent reading room—is a trove of special collections, archives, ephemera, art exhibits and programs that bring our borough's rich history to Brooklynites of all ages." (Via bklynlibrary.org)
properties.note: Closes 4PM Saturday. Othmer Library is closed to the public on Mondays and hours vary from the Center for Brooklyn History building hours.

properties.name: Center for Jewish History
properties.description: "The Center for Jewish History in New York City illuminates history, culture, and heritage. The Center provides a collaborative home for five partner organizations: American Jewish Historical Society, American Sephardi Federation, Leo Baeck Institute, Yeshiva University Museum, and YIVO Institute for Jewish Research." (Via cjh.org)
properties.note: Closes 3:30PM on Friday. Visitors are encouraged to register for advance tickets.

properties.name: Federal Hall National Memorial
properties.description: "The building serves as a museum and memorial to our first President and the beginnings of the United States of America." (via nps.gov)

properties.name: Fort Wadsworth
properties.description: "Fort Wadsworth is one of the oldest military installations in the nation. Rich in history and natural beauty, Fort Wadsworth affords you the opportunity to observe an important part of our nation's history, while offering magnificent views of New York Harbor." (Via nps.gov)

properties.name: General Grant National Memorial
properties.description: "The final resting place of President Ulysses S. Grant and his wife, Julia, is the largest mausoleum in North America. It testifies to a people’s gratitude for the man who ended the bloodiest conflict in American history as Commanding General of the Union Army and then, as President of the United States, strove to heal a nation after a civil war and make rights for all citizens a reality." (Via nps.gov)

properties.name: Hamilton Grange
properties.description: "Merchant’s clerk, Revolutionary War soldier, first Secretary Treasury of the United States: Alexander Hamilton’s life is memorialized at The Grange, the home he built on a country estate in uptown Manhattan. A faithful testament to early American architecture, it stands today as a tribute to its first owner’s many accomplishments." (Via nps.gov)

properties.name: Institute for the Study of the Ancient World
properties.description: "ISAW is a center for advanced scholarly research and graduate education, which aims to encourage particularly the study of the economic, religious, political and cultural connections between ancient civilizations." (Via isaw.nyu.edu)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Friday. Gallery is only open when a show is on view; check website for details.

properties.name: King Manor Museum
properties.description: "King Manor Museum interprets founding father Rufus King’s political legacy and antislavery history to teach critical thinking for a healthier democracy." (Via kingmanor.org)

properties.name: Lewis Latimer House Museum
properties.description: "Latimer House was the home of African-American inventor and electrical pioneer Lewis Howard Latimer from 1903 until his death in 1928." (Via historichousetrust.org)

properties.name: Maritime Industry Museum
properties.description: "Housed in historic Fort Schuyler on the campus of SUNY Maritime College in the Bronx, NY, the Maritime Industry Museum is a family adventure, an educational resource, and a testament to the importance shipping and the seafaring way of life has on our modern global society." (Via nymaritimemuseum.org)

properties.name: Museum of Jewish Heritage
properties.description: "The Museum of Jewish Heritage – A Living Memorial to the Holocaust is New York’s contribution to the global responsibility to never forget. The Museum is committed to the crucial mission of educating diverse visitors about Jewish life before, during, and after the Holocaust." (Via mjhnyc.org)
properties.note: Must reserve free tickets in advance.

properties.name: Museum of Jewish Heritage
properties.description: "The Museum of Jewish Heritage – A Living Memorial to the Holocaust is New York’s contribution to the global responsibility to never forget. The Museum is committed to the crucial mission of educating diverse visitors about Jewish life before, during, and after the Holocaust." (Via mjhnyc.org)
properties.note: Closing hours vary, check website.

properties.name: New York Public Library Stephen A. Schwarzman Building
properties.description: "The New York Public Library’s flagship location, the Stephen A. Schwarzman Building, is one of the Library’s premier research centers, renowned for its extraordinary historical collections and its commitment to providing free and equal access to its resources." (Via nypl.org)
properties.note: Hours vary, check website. Main collection is located at the Visitor Center.

properties.name: Old Stone House of Brooklyn
properties.description:
"Originally built by Claes Vechte in 1699, this reconstructed house, with its solid stone walls and high brick gables, is a landmark in American military and sports history. Today it is the center of a vibrant, diverse community of engaged citizens." (Via historichousetrust.org)

Suggested admission is $3.

properties.name: Rose Museum at Carnegie Hall
properties.description: "The Rose Museum chronicles Carnegie Hall’s history. It exhibits its archival treasures to the public—including concert programs, photographs, autographed posters, musical manuscripts, and video—to tell the history of the building and the events that made it famous." (Via carnegiehall.org)
properties.note: Hours may vary, check website.

properties.name: The Harbor Defense Museum
properties.description: "The mission of the Harbor Defense Museum of New York City is to collect, preserve, exhibit, and interpret historically significant material related to the history of Fort Hamilton and the seacoast defense in New York." (Via history.army.mil)

properties.name: Theodore Roosevelt Birthplace
properties.description: "This is the boyhood home of the first U.S. president to be born in New York City." (Via nps.gov)
properties.note: Upper floors only accessible through the free guided tour.

properties.name: Waterfront Museum
properties.description: "See the captain’s original living quarters, the tools used by longshoremen and other waterfront workers, and the gongs and bells of the Port of New York maritime network. Docents tell the story of the barge’s rescue from the mud and kids love the ball machine that whirls, jumps and plays music via gravity." (Via waterfrontmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open most Saturdays 1-5PM and Thursdays 4-8PM, check website.

properties.name: Weeksville Heritage Center
properties.description: "Weeksville Heritage Center is an historic site and cultural center in Central Brooklyn that uses education, arts and a social justice lens to preserve, document and inspire engagement with the history of Weeksville, one of the largest free Black communities in pre-Civil War America, and the Historic Hunterfly Road Houses." (Via weeksvillesociety.org)
properties.note: Opens 11AM Saturday.

properties.name: Wyckoff House Museum
properties.description: "The Wyckoff House & Association, Inc., was first formed in 1937 when a large group of descendants of Pieter Claesen Wyckoff and Grietje Van Ness-Wyckoff gathered together to purchase and preserve their ancestral home, the Pieter Claesen Wyckoff House." (Via wyckoffmuseum.org)
properties.note: Listed hours are for Drop-in for Guided House Tours, which take place on Saturdays and select Sundays.

properties.name: The Morgan Library & Museum
properties.description: "A museum and independent research library located in the heart of New York City, the Morgan Library & Museum began as the personal library of financier, collector, and cultural benefactor Pierpont Morgan. As early as 1890 Morgan had begun to assemble a collection of illuminated, literary, and historical manuscripts, early printed books, and old master drawings and prints." (Via Themorgan.org)
properties.note: Reservations are required. Tickets are available one week in advance.

properties.name: The Morgan Library & Museum
properties.description: "A museum and independent research library located in the heart of New York City, the Morgan Library & Museum began as the personal library of financier, collector, and cultural benefactor Pierpont Morgan. As early as 1890 Morgan had begun to assemble a collection of illuminated, literary, and historical manuscripts, early printed books, and old master drawings and prints." (Via Themorgan.org)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Fridays.

properties.name: The New York Historical
properties.description: "Groundbreaking exhibitions, outstanding collections, and immersive films." (Via nyhistory.org)
properties.note: Tickets are pay what you wish.

properties.name: The New York Historical
properties.description: "Groundbreaking exhibitions, outstanding collections, and immersive films." (Via nyhistory.org)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Fridays.

properties.name: Fordham Museum of Greek, Etruscan and Roman Art
properties.description: "The antiquities collection at Fordham University is chronologically expansive and typologically diverse, with objects ranging in date from 3400 B.C.E. to the 5th century C.E. and made in every type of medium. Our goal is to present a holistic view of the ancient world through its material culture." (Via library.fordham.edu)
properties.note: Opens 10AM Sunday. Hours may vary outside of the academic year.

properties.name: The Skyscraper Museum
properties.description: "Located in New York City, the world's first and foremost vertical metropolis, The Skyscraper Museum celebrates the City's rich architectural heritage and examines the historical forces and individuals that have shaped its successive skylines." (Via skyscraper.org)
properties.note: Advance tickets reccomended.

properties.name: Brooklyn Children's Museum
properties.description: "Inspired by the energy of our borough, Brooklyn Children’s Museum creates experiences that ignite curiosity, celebrate identity and cultivate joyful learning." (Via Brooklynkids.org)
properties.note: Tickets can be reserved in advance online, as well as at BCM on the day-of. Group visits are restricted during this time.

properties.name: Brooklyn Children's Museum
properties.description: "Inspired by the energy of our borough, Brooklyn Children’s Museum creates experiences that ignite curiosity, celebrate identity and cultivate joyful learning." (Via Brooklynkids.org)

properties.name: New York Hall of Science
properties.description: "NYSCI’s mission is to nurture passionate learners, critical thinkers, and active citizens through an approach we call Design Make Play." (Via nysci.org)
properties.note: Tickets can be reserved starting on 10am the day of your visit.

properties.name: New York Hall of Science
properties.description: "NYSCI’s mission is to nurture passionate learners, critical thinkers, and active citizens through an approach we call Design Make Play." (Via nysci.org)

properties.name: Bronx Zoo
properties.description: "The Bronx Zoo, the Wildlife Conservation Society’s flagship park, opened on November 8, 1899. Since then, the zoo has created a unique window into the natural world for city dwellers in the heart of the Bronx - and serves as a springboard and hub for conservation efforts across the entire planet." (Via Bronxzoo.com)
properties.note: Advance online reservation for tickets required. The Wednesday Bronx Zoo Tickets Store will open Monday afternoon at 5pm for that Wednesday’s reservations.

properties.name: New York Aquarium
properties.description: "Discover the sea that never sleeps in the waters right across the boardwalk. Or take in one of Coney Island’s best shows at the Aquatheater. The Aquarium is home to sharks, rays, sea otters, octopus, jellyfish and more. Plan a visit to explore for yourself." (Via nyaquarium.org)
properties.note: Advance online reservation for tickets required. The Wednesday Bronx Zoo Tickets Store will open Monday afternoon at 5pm for that Wednesday’s reservations.

properties.name: Staten Island Zoo
properties.description: The Staten Island Zoo is an 8-acre urban zoo in West New Brighton, Staten Island, New York City. (via wikipedia.org)

properties.name: BronxArtSpace
properties.description: "BronxArtSpace is a community-based organization committed to supporting local and often under-resourced artists, fostering projects that engage vital social, educational, and political concerns." (via bronxartspace.com)
properties.note: Open 12-5PM Saturday.

properties.name: Derfner Judaica Museum
properties.description: "[Our] mission is to collect and display fine art and Jewish ceremonial objects and to interpret its collections for the public." (via derfner.org)
properties.note: Photo ID is required for admission.

properties.name: Longwood Art Gallery
properties.description: "The Longwood Arts Project is a flagship program of the Bronx Council on the Arts (BCA). Founded in 1981, it was one of the first alternative gallery spaces in The Bronx and to this day maintains its pioneering focus on artists whose traditions and cultural practices are underrepresented by mainstream venues." (via bronxarts.org)

properties.name: The AKC Museum of the Dog
properties.description: "The AKC Museum of the Dog preserves, interprets, and celebrates the role of dogs in society and educates the public about the human-canine bond through its collection of art and exhibits that inspire engagement with dogs." (via museumofthedog.org)

properties.name: Alice Austen House
properties.description: "The Alice Austen House fosters creative expression, explores personal identity, and educates and inspires the public through the interpretation of the photographs, life and historic home of American photographer, Alice Austen." (via aliceausten.org)
properties.note: Opens 11AM Saturday.

properties.name: Alice Austen House
properties.description: "The Alice Austen House fosters creative expression, explores personal identity, and educates and inspires the public through the interpretation of the photographs, life and historic home of American photographer, Alice Austen." (via aliceausten.org)
properties.note: Opens 11AM Saturday.

properties.name: Bartow-Pell Mansion Museum
properties.description: "As the only grand 19th-century country house still in existence on Pelham Bay, the museum provides an important link to the social and architectural history of New York." (via bartowpellmansionmuseum.org)

properties.name: Bronx Children's Museum
properties.description: "At the Bronx Children's Museum, we nurture the wonder of childhood, fostering curiosity, creativity and a lifelong love for learning through interactive STEAM experiences." (via bxcm.org)
properties.note: Additional hours on Saturday from 2-5PM.

properties.name: Children’s Museum of Manhattan
properties.description: "For more than 50 years, the Children’s Museum of Manhattan has been a trusted resource for families and caregivers, filling gaps in educational services for children in New York City and beyond." (via cmom.org)
properties.note: Also open on Holiday Mondays.

properties.name: Fraunces Tavern Museum
properties.description: "Fraunces Tavern Museum’s mission is to preserve and interpret the history of the American Revolutionary era through public education." (via frauncestavernmuseum.org)

properties.name: Historic Richmond Town
properties.description: "Historic Richmond Town interprets and restores a living history village, operates a historical museum, and collects and preserves material culture to inspire engagement with and understanding of the diverse histories of Staten Island." (via historicrichmondtown.org)
properties.note: From January-March, site is closed on Wednesday and Thursday.

properties.name: Intrepid Museum
properties.description: "Experience the world’s first space shuttle, a nuclear weapons-carrying submarine, dozens of military aircraft, a supersonic spy plane, and the world’s fastest commercial airliner displayed in and around the legendary aircraft carrier, Intrepid—an awe-inspiring place for an unforgettable adventure." (via intrepidmuseum.org)

properties.name: The Museum of Modern Art
properties.description: "MoMA's collection spans the late 19th century to the present, and includes over 200,000 works of architecture and design, drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, prints, illustrated and artist's books, film, as well as electronic media." (via wikipedia.org)
properties.note: Tickets must be reserved in advance, and are subject to availability and proof of residency.

properties.name: The Museum of Modern Art
properties.description: "MoMA's collection spans the late 19th century to the present, and includes over 200,000 works of architecture and design, drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, prints, illustrated and artist's books, film, as well as electronic media." (via wikipedia.org)
properties.note: Open until 8:30PM Friday nights.

properties.name: Studio Museum in Harlem
properties.description: "The Studio Museum in Harlem is the nexus for artists of African descent locally, nationally, and internationally and for work that has been inspired and influenced by Black culture." (via studiomuseum.org)
properties.note: Advanced ticket reservations are recommended.

properties.name: Studio Museum in Harlem
properties.description: "The Studio Museum in Harlem is the nexus for artists of African descent locally, nationally, and internationally and for work that has been inspired and influenced by Black culture." (via studiomuseum.org)
properties.note: Open until 9PM Friday and Saturday

properties.name: Sugar Hill Children's Museum of Art & Storytelling
properties.description: "The Sugar Hill Children’s Museum of Art & Storytelling provides our culturally rich neighborhood with a space where children and their families grow and learn about Sugar Hill, and about the world at large, through intergenerational dialogue with artists, art and storytelling." (via sugarhillmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open until 5PM Fridays.

properties.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
properties.description: "The Metropolitan Museum of Art collects, studies, conserves, and presents significant works of art across time and cultures in order to connect all people to creativity, knowledge, ideas, and one another." (via metmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open until 9PM on Friday and Saturday. Pay-What-You-Wish admission for New York State residents and New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut students.

properties.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
properties.description: "The Metropolitan Museum of Art collects, studies, conserves, and presents significant works of art across time and cultures in order to connect all people to creativity, knowledge, ideas, and one another." (via metmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open until 9PM on Friday and Saturday.

properties.name: The Noble Maritime Collection
properties.description: "The mission of the Noble Maritime Collection is to present exhibitions and programs that celebrate the working waterfront of New York Harbor in the tradition of distinguished artist John A. Noble (1913-1983), and to preserve Robbins Reef Lighthouse and the history of Sailors’ Snug Harbor." (via noblemaritime.org)
properties.note: Admission is Pay-What-You-Wish.

properties.name: Staten Island Museum
properties.description: "It is the mission of the Staten Island Museum to spark curiosity and generate meaningful shared experiences through natural science, art, and history to deepen understanding of our environment, ourselves, and each other." (via statenislandmuseum.org)

properties.name: Van Cortlandt House Museum
properties.description: "Van Cortlandt House Museum is the centerpiece of a 1,000-acre urban park that encompasses what was once the Van Cortlandt family’s plantation." (via vchm.org)
properties.note: Also free for all Bronx residents.

properties.name: American Museum of Natural History
properties.description: "Since its founding in 1869, the Museum has advanced its global mission to discover, interpret, and disseminate information about human cultures, the natural world, and the universe through a wide-ranging program of scientific research, education, and exhibition." (via amnh.org)

properties.name: American Museum of Natural History
properties.description: "Since its founding in 1869, the Museum has advanced its global mission to discover, interpret, and disseminate information about human cultures, the natural world, and the universe through a wide-ranging program of scientific research, education, and exhibition." (via amnh.org)
properties.note: Pay-What-You-Wish for residents of NY State.

properties.name: American Museum of Natural History
properties.description: "Since its founding in 1869, the Museum has advanced its global mission to discover, interpret, and disseminate information about human cultures, the natural world, and the universe through a wide-ranging program of scientific research, education, and exhibition." (via amnh.org)

properties.name: Bard Graduate Center Gallery
properties.description: "The Bard Graduate Center Gallery produces multiple exhibitions and publications each year, serving as a vital center of learning and a catalyst for engagement in the interrelated disciplines of decorative arts, design, and material culture." (via bgc.bard.edu)
properties.note: Free the first Friday of every month.

properties.name: Bard Graduate Center Gallery
properties.description: "The Bard Graduate Center Gallery produces multiple exhibitions and publications each year, serving as a vital center of learning and a catalyst for engagement in the interrelated disciplines of decorative arts, design, and material culture." (via bgc.bard.edu)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Wednesdays.

properties.name: Brooklyn Botanic Garden
properties.description: "Brooklyn Botanic Garden connects people to the wonder and power of plants, sparking delight and curiosity about science, culture, and our environment to help create a greener, more sustainable city and world." (via bbg.org)

properties.name: Center for Book Arts
properties.description: "Center for Book Arts promotes active explorations of artistic practices related to the book as an art object." (via centerforbookarts.org)
properties.note: Closes 5PM Friday and Saturday.

properties.name: Dyckman Farmhouse Museum
properties.description: "The mission of DFMA is to support the preservation of the historic site, to be a catalyst for engaging, adventuresome programming and to be a good neighbor and a dynamic resource for the community." (via dyckmanfarmhouse.org)
properties.note: Opens 10AM Saturday. Advanced ticket purchase highly recommended.

properties.name: Museum of Bronx History
properties.description: "The main level of the museum contains three galleries that feature a rich rotation of exhibitions as well as a museum gift store." (via bronxhistoricalsociety.org)
properties.note: Open 1-5PM Sunday.

properties.name: Edgar Allan Poe Cottage
properties.description: "The historic house museum is famous as the final home of the writer." (via bronxhistoricalsociety.org)
properties.note: Open 1-5PM Sunday.

properties.name: The International Center Photography
properties.description: "The International Center of Photography is the world’s leading institution dedicated to photography and visual culture. Cornell Capa founded ICP in 1974 to champion “concerned photography”—socially and politically minded images that can educate and change the world." (via icp.org)
properties.note: Free the third Thursday of every month. RSVP required.

properties.name: The International Center Photography
properties.description: "The International Center of Photography is the world’s leading institution dedicated to photography and visual culture. Cornell Capa founded ICP in 1974 to champion “concerned photography”—socially and politically minded images that can educate and change the world." (via icp.org)
properties.note: Open until 8PM Thursdays.

properties.name: International Studio & Curatorial Program
properties.description: "For over three decades, ISCP has been a New York City hub for creativity, supporting artists and curators from around the world." (via iscp-nyc.org)

properties.name: The Jackie Robinson Museum
properties.description: "The Jackie Robinson Museum celebrates the enduring legacy of one of the most important Americans of the 20th century, chronicling his trailblazing achievements against the backdrop of United States history." (via jackierobinsonmuseum.org)

properties.name: Jacques Marchais Museum of Tibetan Art
properties.description: "The mission of Jacques Marchais Museum of Tibetan Art is to preserve its unique collection of rare and sacred artifacts, provide careful stewardship of its historic buildings and gardens, and present the art, culture and history of Tibet to a world audience, educating and inspiring appreciation of Himalayan cultures and fostering better global understanding." (via tibetanmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open 1-5PM Thursday and Friday.

properties.name: The Met Cloisters
properties.description: "Art, architecture, and gardens of medieval Europe." (via metmuseum.org)
properties.note: Pay-What-You-Wish admission for New York State residents and New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut students.

properties.name: The Met Cloisters
properties.description: "Art, architecture, and gardens of medieval Europe." (via metmuseum.org)

properties.name: Museum of Arts and Design
properties.description: "The mission of the Museum of Arts and Design is to collect, display, and interpret objects that document contemporary and historic innovation in craft, art, and design. In its exhibitions and educational programs, the Museum celebrates the creative process through which materials are crafted into works that enhance contemporary life." (Via madmuseum.org)
properties.note: Open 12-8PM Thursdays.

properties.name: National Lighthouse Museum
properties.description: "The National Lighthouse Museum is located on the grounds of the former U.S. Lighthouse Service "Super" Depot on the North Shore of Staten Island." (via lighthousemuseum.org)

properties.name: New York Transit Museum
properties.description: "A one-of-a-kind experience, the New York Transit Museum is housed in a decommissioned subway station at 99 Schermerhorn St in Brooklyn, and is accessible via more than 20 subway and bus lines." (via nytransitmuseum.org)

properties.name: Queens Botanical Garden
properties.description: "Queens Botanical Garden is an urban oasis where people, plants, and cultures are celebrated through inspiring gardens, innovative educational programs, and real-world applications of environmental stewardship." (queensbotanical.org)
properties.note: Wednesday free hours are 3-6PM; Sunday free hours are 9-11AM. Also free all of Dec-Mar. Hours vary, check website.

properties.name: Queens Botanical Garden
properties.description: "Queens Botanical Garden is an urban oasis where people, plants, and cultures are celebrated through inspiring gardens, innovative educational programs, and real-world applications of environmental stewardship." (queensbotanical.org)
properties.note: Hours vary seasonally, check website.

properties.name: The Society of Illustrators
properties.description: "The Society of Illustrators’ mission is to promote the art of illustration, to appreciate its history and evolving nature through exhibitions, lectures and education, and to contribute the service of its members to the welfare of the community at large." (via societyillustrators.org)

properties.name: Ukraninian Museum
properties.description: "The Museum’s purpose is to acquire, preserve, exhibit, and interpret articles of artistic or historic value related to Ukrainian life and culture; to promote all aspects of Ukrainian culture; and to educate the public about Ukraine and Ukrainian culture." (via theukrainianmuseum.org)

properties.name: Prospect Park Audubon Center
properties.description: "Prospect Park Alliance offers free public programming that allows park visitors of all ages to explore the diversity of nature in Prospect Park at the Prospect Park Audubon Center. This first urban-area Audubon Center in the nation is the result of a partnership forged in 2000 between the Prospect Park Alliance and Audubon New York, the state office for National Audubon Society." (via prospectpark.org)

properties.name: The Cathedral Church of Saint John The Divine
properties.description: "The Cathedral of St. John the Divine is the world's largest Gothic Cathedral, combining soaring architecture with intimate spaces that inspire awe and reflection." (via stjohndivine.org)
properties.note: Opens at 12PM Sunday.

properties.name: Central Park Zoo
properties.description: "Manhattan’s Central Park Zoo is one of the oldest in the country, first opening on its current site in 1861. In the 1980s, the Wildlife Conservation Society assumed management of the park under a contract with the City of New York and completed a dramatic five year transformation. Since reopening in 1988, our mission has been to engage future generations of conservationists and create wildlife champions." (via centralparkzoo.com)
properties.note: Hours vary seasonally, check website. Use promo code COOLC during checkout. Must present valid Cool Culture card and an ID that matches the last name on that Cool Culture card on entry along with ticket(s).

properties.name: FDNY Fire Zone
properties.description: "The FDNY Fire Zone is New York City’s state-of-the-art fire-safety learning center. At the Fire Zone, you can climb on a realistic fire truck, try on bunker gear, meet a firefighter, and learn how to crawl through a smoke-filled hallway." (via fdnysmart.org)

properties.name: Merchant’s House Museum
properties.description: "Through public education programs and exhibitions, restoration of its 1832 landmark building, and conservation of its original collections, the Museum tells the story of the domestic life of a wealthy merchant family and their four Irish servants in the mid-19th century, when the mercantile seaport of New York City emerged as a growing metropolis and the commercial emporium of America." (via merchantshouse,org)

properties.name: Mount Vernon Hotel Museum & Garden
properties.description: "The Mount Vernon Hotel Museum & Garden presents the period of the Mount Vernon Hotel which operated from 1826 until 1833." (via mvhm.org)
properties.note: Reservations are not required; tickets can be purchased in advance on Eventbrite or in the shop shortly prior to the tour start time. Visits are by guided tour only.

properties.name: The Paley Museum
properties.description: "Through its respected programming, the Paley Center leads the discussion about the cultural, creative, and social significance of media, drawing upon its curatorial expertise, an international collection, and close relationships with the media community." (via paleycenter.org)

properties.name: South Street Seaport Museum
properties.description: "The South Street Seaport Museum, located in the heart of the historic Seaport District, preserves and interprets the history of New York as a great port city." (via southstreetseaportmuseum.org)
properties.note: Hours vary, check website.

properties.name: Louis Armstrong House Museum
properties.description: "The Louis Armstrong House Museum sustains and promotes the cultural, historical, and humanitarian legacy of Louis Armstrong by preserving and interpreting Armstrong’s house and grounds, collecting and sharing archival materials that document Armstrong’s life and legacy, developing programs for the public that educate and inspire, and engaging with contemporary artists to create performances and new works." (via louisarmstronghouse.org)

properties.name: Queens Historical Society
properties.description: "The Queens Historical Society is the largest and most active historical society in the borough and the only one with a borough-wide scope and impact." (via queenshistoricalsociety.org)
properties.note: Entry is by advance timed ticket only.

properties.name: Queens Zoo
properties.description: "Discover South America’s spectacled bears or the smallest deer species before getting up-close with domestic animals." (via queenszoo.com)
properties.note: Hours vary seasonally. Reserve tickets through Cool Culture website.

properties.name: Staten Island Children’s Museum
properties.description: "Expand your mind as you explore our exhibits, create art in our workshop, investigate reasons why, and interact with performers in a show." (via sichildrensmuseum.org)

properties.name: The Agnes Varis Art Center
properties.description: "The Agnes Varis Art Center is a space for the public to see and learn about cutting edge art and design in glass." (via urbanglass.org)
properties.note: Closes 5PM Sundays.

properties.name: Coney Island History Project
properties.description: "Visitors to the exhibition center are invited to take free souvenir photos with Coney Island's only original Steeplechase horse, from the legendary ride that gave Steeplechase Park its name, and the iconic Cyclops head from Deno's Wonder Wheel Park's Spook-A-Rama, Coney Island's oldest dark ride." (via coneyislandhistory.org)

properties.name: FiveMyles
properties.description: "Our mission is to advance public interest in innovative experimental work; to identify and exhibit the work of under-represented artists, and to engage the local community through participation in the Arts." (via fivemyles.org)

properties.name: Green-Wood Cemetery
properties.description: "Green-Wood is a living cemetery that brings people closer to the world as it is and was, by memorializing the dead and bringing to life the art, history, and natural beauty of New York City." (via green-wood.com)

properties.name: Kentler International Drawing Space
properties.description: "Kentler International Drawing Space is a nonprofit organization dedicated to bringing contemporary drawings and works on paper by emerging and under-recognized national and international artists to the public, and to providing the opportunity to experiment, explore and expand the definition of art in society." (via kentlergallery.org)
properties.note: Only open during exhibitions, check website.

properties.name: Pioneer Works
properties.description: "Pioneer Works is an artist and scientist-led nonprofit cultural center in Red Hook, Brooklyn that fosters innovative thinking through the visual and performing arts, technology, music, and science." (via pioneerworks.org)

properties.name: Admant
properties.description: "The core of Amant’s mission is to advance the presentation of contemporary art with an emphasis on innovative formats and ideas." (via amant.org)
properties.note: Open until 4PM Thursday–Sunday.

properties.name: Williamsburg Biannual
properties.description: "The Williamsburg Biannual celebrates the diversity and creativity of the artists and designers practicing in New York City through programs and exhibitions designed to make art accessible to people of all backgrounds, genders, races and socioeconomic status." (via williamsburgbiannual.org)
properties.note: Admission by appointment only.

properties.name: Abrons Arts Center
properties.description: "Abrons Arts Center builds a community where artists, learners, and audiences explore creative possibilities. Rooted in the immigrant and working-class history of our Lower East Side neighborhood, we offer free and affordable exhibitions, performances, classes, residencies, and space access." (via abronsartscenter.org)

properties.name: The Arsenal Gallery
properties.description: The Arsenal Gallery is a public art space located in the historic Arsenal Building.

properties.name: CUE Art Foundation
properties.description: "CUE Art  is a nonprofit organization that works with and for emerging and underrecognized artists and art workers to create new opportunities and present varied perspectives in the arts." (via cueartfoundation.org)

properties.name: Denny Farrell Greenhouse and Education Center
properties.description: "The Denny Farrell Greenhouse and Education Center is the collective backyard of Harlem that welcomes everyone with an early curiosity to the deepest passion for gardening. Anyone from the community can enjoy public programs and classes for free." (via thehort.org)

properties.name: The Elizabeth Foundation for the Arts
properties.description: "The Elizabeth Foundation for the Arts is a public charity, dedicated to providing artists across all disciplines with space, tools and a cooperative forum for the development of individual practice. We are a catalyst for cultural growth, stimulating new interactions between artists, creative communities, and the public." (via efanyc.org)
properties.note: Hours vary by exhibition, check website.

properties.name: Horological Society of New York
properties.description: The Horological society showcases the art, science, and history of timekeeping.

properties.name: White Columns
properties.description: "New York’s oldest alternative art space." (via whitecolumns.org)
properties.note: Hours vary seasonally, check website.

properties.name: Culture Lab LIC
properties.description: "Culture Lab LIC is the arts and culture umbrella for Western Queens. We present local, national, and international art of all genres, while supporting New York artists and other nonprofits by providing space, resources and a sense of community." (via culturelablic.org)
properties.note: Opens at 2pm on weekends.

properties.name: Jamaica Center for Arts & Learning
properties.description: "Jamaica Center for Arts and Learning is a multidisciplinary arts center based in the diverse community of Southeast Queens. Our mission is to offer quality visual and performing arts programs and to provide accessible education programs to encourage participation in the arts." (via jcal.org)

properties.name: Materials for the Arts
properties.description: "Materials for the Arts provides NYC arts nonprofits, public schools and city agencies with access to free materials. We strive to keep valuable materials from entering the landfill and put these materials into the hands of arts professionals, educators and students across the five boroughs." (via materialsforthearts.org)

properties.name: Art Lab
properties.description: "Through our mission Art Lab is dedicated to providing opportunities for exhibitions of the work of emerging artists, established artists, students, faculty, and community groups." (via artlabsi.com)
properties.note: Open until 7pm Mon-Thu.
